# Routing Attacks and Mitigation Methods for RPL-Based Internet of Things

## Summary
The paper investigates routing attacks and mitigation methods for RPL (Routing Protocol for Low-Power and Lossy Networks) used in IoT environments. It provides a thorough review of RPL specifications and discusses a recent proposal to enhance its security. The authors analyze the RPL protocol stack, compare it to the traditional internet protocol stack, and suggest security improvements to address vulnerabilities in IoT networks.

## Contributions
- Comprehensive review of RPL specifications and security-minded proposals.
- Analysis of RPL protocol stack and its vulnerabilities in IoT environments.
- Suggestions for security improvements to mitigate routing attacks in IoT networks.

## Method
The authors conducted a detailed review of the RPL protocol, including its specifications and recent security proposals. They compared the RPL protocol stack with the traditional internet protocol stack and analyzed its vulnerabilities in security-sensitive IoT environments.

## Results
The paper highlights the need for enhanced security measures in RPL-based IoT networks and proposes modifications to the protocol to mitigate routing attacks. The analysis underscores the importance of addressing security vulnerabilities in IoT environments.

## Limitations
The content of the paper is subject to change before final publication, and some details may be refined. Further research is needed to fully validate the proposed security improvements.

## Keywords
RPL, IoT, Routing Attacks, Mitigation Methods, Security, 6LoWPAN, Protocol Stack, Vulnerabilities
